seoTitle: How to Enable TradingView on Tradovate for Direct Trading
metaDescription: See how to turn on the TradingView Add-On inside Tradovate, link your accounts, and place Market, Limit, and OCO orders straight from your TradingView chart.
intro: Trading from TradingView charts while executing through Tradovate's zero-commission futures platform takes just a few settings changes. This guide walks through turning on the TradingView Add-On in your Tradovate subscription, linking the two accounts, and confirming everything works before you risk real money. You'll also see where the $9.99/month add-on pricing fits in and how to test the connection safely first.

steps.heading: Turn on the TradingView Add-On in Tradovate
steps.body: Open your Tradovate subscription settings and locate the TradingView Integration option to activate the add-on, which runs $9.99 per month on top of your existing plan.

steps.heading: Link your TradingView and Tradovate accounts
steps.body: Connect the two platforms securely so TradingView can send order instructions straight to your Tradovate brokerage account.

steps.heading: Place trades from the TradingView chart
steps.body: Once linked, use TradingView's charting tools to submit Market, Limit, and OCO orders that execute through Tradovate without switching platforms.

steps.heading: Test the setup with Paper Trading first
steps.body: Before trading live, run through a few orders in Paper Trading mode to confirm the integration is working the way you expect.

faq.question: Does the TradingView Add-On cost extra on Tradovate?
faq.answer: Yes, the TradingView Integration add-on is priced separately at around $9.99 per month in addition to your regular Tradovate subscription.

faq.question: Can I place Market, Limit, and OCO orders from TradingView?
faq.answer: Yes, once the add-on is enabled and your accounts are linked, you can submit Market, Limit, and OCO orders directly from the TradingView chart.

faq.question: Is it safe to link my TradingView account to Tradovate?
faq.answer: The integration is designed to securely connect the two accounts so order instructions pass through without exposing your login credentials.

faq.question: Should I test the integration before trading live?
faq.answer: Yes, using Paper Trading first lets you confirm orders route correctly before you commit real funds.
